images.alt:
A stunningly stylized, oversized orange worm lounges in blissful contentment on the gentle curve of a perfectly ripe, golden-yellow banana. The worm is magnificent—its body is a luminous, vibrant orange with a glossy, almost wet sheen, segmented into plump, rounded sections that catch the warm sunlight. Each segment is adorned with intricate, hand-painted markings in deeper burnt orange, amber, and hints of coral—delicate geometric patterns, swirling mandalas, and tiny, spiraling dots that suggest both ancient tribal art and contemporary design. Its skin texture is smooth yet detailed, with subtle ridges and tiny, glistening pores.

The worm lies on its back, belly exposed to the sun, in a pose of pure, lazy relaxation. Its face, turned slightly toward the viewer, wears an expression of blissful contentment—large, expressive eyes half-closed in pleasure, a tiny, curved smile on its lips. Its small, stubby legs are splayed outward in complete surrender to the warmth.

The banana beneath it is a perfect specimen—rich, buttery yellow with natural brown speckles suggesting peak ripeness, its curved form providing an ideal lounging spot. The banana's skin has subtle textures, tiny striations, and a soft, matte sheen that contrasts beautifully with the worm's glossy orange.

The setting is a sun-drenched tropical paradise—soft, out-of-focus palm fronds in the background, dappled sunlight filtering through, tiny droplets of water on the banana's surface suggesting recent mist. The lighting is warm and golden, creating a soft halo around the worm and casting gentle shadows.

The mood is one of pure, whimsical joy—a celebration of lazy afternoons, vibrant color, and the simple pleasure of sunbathing on a banana. Every intricate marking, every glistening segment, every speck on the banana is rendered with insane, breathtaking precision. Whimsical atmosphere.

¿Qué pasa dentro de las zonas más ocultas de una red neuronal como la del ChatGPT?

Durante años, las redes neuronales han sido tratadas como cajas negras: sistemas capaces de aprender, pero difíciles de interpretar desde dentro. Sin embargo, parte de la investigación más reciente intenta mirar con…

external.description: Durante años, las redes neuronales han sido tratadas como cajas negras: sistemas capaces de aprender, pero difíciles de interpretar desde dentro. Sin embargo, parte de la investigación más reciente intenta mirar con mayor detalle qué ocurre en ese proceso. La idea de fondo es sencilla, aunque sus implicaciones no lo sean: estos modelos aprenden ajustando millones de parámetros internos llamados pesos, y esos cambios microscópicos pueden ir dejando huellas más amplias en la estructura de la red. Un artículo reciente propone justamente una teoría para conectar esos cambios pequeños, que ocurren durante el entrenamiento, con patrones globales que aparecen en las matrices de pesos del modelo.
external.title: ¿Qué pasa dentro de las zonas más ocultas de una red neuronal como la del ChatGPT?
